channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KREX 5

item.title: Family of Sonya Massey, Black woman shot in home by sheriff's deputy, accepts $10M settlement
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — County officials in Springfield, Illinois, have agreed to pay $10 million to the family of Sonya Massey, a 36-year-old Black woman shot and killed in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy responding to her call for help.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inflation ticks up to 3 percent annual increase in January
item.description: The consumer price index (CPI) increased by 0.5 percent in January to hit an annual increase of 3.0 percent after advancing by 2.9 percent in December.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne's 'final show' with original Black Sabbath: What we know
item.description: The performance, called "The Back to the Beginning," will cap off an all-day event featuring sets from several iconic heavy metal acts.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House fires USAID inspector general following critical report
item.description: Under the law, the administration is required to give 30-day notice to Congress prior to terminating an inspector general and give reasons why.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: AP says reporter blocked from White House over ‘Gulf of Mexico’ policy
item.description: The barring of the journalist, the AP said in a statement, was in connection with AP's refusal to "align its editorial standards with President Donald Trump’s executive order renaming the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Snow and freezing rain cause crashes in Virginia while California prepares for likely flooding
item.description: Heavy snow and freezing rain caused hundreds of crashes in Virginia and left tens of thousands of residents in the dark as the central Appalachians and parts of the mid-Atlantic states are bracing for heavy rains.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, made a rare public appearance at the White House on Tuesday to defend the swift and extensive cuts he's pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there have been mistakes and will be more.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US special envoy says another American hostage set to return home
item.description: Just when one American hostage detained in Russia gets ready to return to the U.S. on Tuesday, officials said another hostage is set to be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Musk joins Trump in Oval Office to tout DOGE
item.description: Despite Musk’s pledge to be “maximally transparent,” the White House on Tuesday fired the inspector general for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KRON4

item.title: Coast Guard detains Mexican fishermen with over 1,600 pounds of illegally caught fish
item.description: U.S. Coast Guard crews intercepted four Mexican boats fishing illegally in federal waters off the coast of Texas, detaining all 11 fishermen and over 1,600 pounds of fish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia residents outsmarted home invaders, stole their getaway car: prosecutor
item.description: Dogs really are a man's best friend – In Norfolk, Virginia one even provided a distraction that allowed a home invasion victim to escape.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as an attacker repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — With a mix of humor and graphic detail, Salman Rushdie calmly told a jury Tuesday about the frenzied moments in August 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with terrible injuries.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins. Trump on Monday announced a 25% tariff on steel and aluminum and promised more import duties to come.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina man pleads guilty to supporting ISIS, prosecutors say
item.description: Alexander Justin White, 29, was arrested on Dec. 4 at Raleigh-Durham International Airport while he was preparing to board an international flight.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court won't halt judge’s order requiring Trump administration to unfreeze all federal cash
item.description: A federal appeals court on Tuesday refused to immediately halt a judge's order requiring the Trump administration to release billions of dollars in federal grants and loans that remain frozen even after a court blocked a sweeping pause on federal funding.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Papa John's debuts bath bomb inspired by its signature garlic sauce
item.description: Papa John’s is giving its fans a new way to enjoy its signature garlic sauce this Valentine’s Day. On Tuesday, the pizza chain announced that they created a limited-edition bath bomb inspired by the famous sauce that comes with every pizza order.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: New York City's mayor has 4 months to persuade Democratic voters he's not Trump's puppet
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams declared Tuesday that he is “no longer facing legal questions” after the Justice Department moved to shield him from the bribery charges that have been hanging over his reelection campaign.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire knocked down, 7 injured in Sun Metro fire
item.description: FINAL UPDATE: Fire knocked down. Crews checking for hot spots. A total of seven patients were assessed- four were transported, and three patients refused. EPFD investigators are on the scene.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-49er Dana Stubblefield freed from prison after rape conviction overturned
item.description: A judge has granted the release of former San Francisco 49er Dana Stubblefield after his 2020 rape conviction was vacated by a California appeals court last December.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Firefighters battle inferno after reported explosion near Texas airport, 7 injured
item.description: Texas firefighters are battling a large fire near El Paso International Airport after a reported explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he'll abide by court orders that block parts of his agenda
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he would abide by court rulings if they blocked parts of his agenda amid uproar over comments from some allies about defying the legal system.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: 10th person charged in 'violent' Aurora apartment kidnapping
item.description: A male and female victim were allegedly tied up, pistol-whipped, beaten and stabbed by about 12 armed individuals, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't block immigration arrests in houses of worship. Now these 27 religious groups are suing
item.description: More than two-dozen Christian and Jewish groups representing millions of Americans — ranging from the Episcopal Church and the Union for Reform Judaism to the Mennonites and Unitarian Universalists — filed a federal court lawsuit Tuesday challenging a Trump administration move giving immigration age
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Most US kids aren't gaining fast enough in reading and math. These schools are different
item.description: COMPTON, Calif. (AP) — Math is the subject sixth grader Harmoni Knight finds hardest, but that's changing. In-class tutors and “data chats” at her middle school in Compton, California, have made a dramatic difference, the 11-year-old said.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amish community helps with Tennessee flood relief
item.description: "We've done a little bit of everything," said Miller. "We've put in some footers. We've laid block. We've framed house with help.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese spy balloon equipped with tech from at least 5 US firms: report
item.description: Chinese spy balloon that floated across the U.S. before being shot down off the South Carolina coast in 2023 was carrying technology from at least five American companies, report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5
